techmeme-2022.text: An in-depth look at the rise and fall of crypto hedge fund Three Arrows Capital, whose founders Su Zhu and Kyle Davies bet everything on prices only going up

Sadly, our good faith to cooperate with the Liquidators was met with baiting. Hope that they did exercise good faith wrt the StarkWare token warrants. https://twitter.com/...

Really good article. Reminds me of the Chuck Prince quote: “When the music stops, in terms of liquidity, things will be complicated. But as long as the music is playing, you've got to get up and dance.” https://twitter.com/...

Think this @hosseeb quote really captures what most struck me writing about 3AC They really practiced what they preached! They really bought the dip, even on margin! In May, when Luna was collapsing, Kyle was still talking about buying BTC and ETH *on margin* https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

Three Arrows was a venture investor in some of the best-known crypto startups and, in some cases, manager of their corporate treasuries. It was both an aggressive borrower from large lenders and a shareholder in some of those lenders https://www.bloomberg.com/...

Three Arrows became the most important hedge fund in crypto. But when the price of Bitcoin fell, the fund's sudden collapse revealed how interconnected the crypto industry is https://www.bloomberg.com/...

I learned recently that the phrase “hedge fund” applies to a specific shape of legal entity and does not require that the entity have any actual hedging strategy, which is how you get a company that works only if crypto keeps going up forever. https://www.bloomberg.com/...

Some Crypto trading platforms have since disclosed exposures to 3AC. One has gone under after 3AC defaulted on a loan worth more than $650 million. Its customers have had their crypto accounts frozen and are unlikely to get all their assets back https://www.bloomberg.com/...

FINALLY!! If you wonder how exactly 3AC turned to crypto's Archegos or Long-Term Capital Management, we investigated and reported from Singapore, Dubai, to London, and New York. w @justinaknope @BenBartenstein via @crypto @BW https://www.bloomberg.com/...

Why I think @zhusu is full of it (1/x): + The Liquidator, Teneo, was appointed late June; Warrant exercise by July 5 + Teneo needs to go through 100s of contracts + positions w/ context of 3ACs biz + They likely triaged liquid assets first as Su/Kyle could run off with those https://twitter.com/...

How Crypto Hedge Fund Three Arrows Capital Fell Apart (3AC) - Bloomberg “Su Zhu and Kyle Davies turned 3AC into the most important hedge fund in crypto, but they bet everything on prices only going up.” That is not a hedge fun, it was a gambling operation https://www.bloomberg.com/...
GREAT READ: Three Arrows bet everything on Bitcoin's rise. When prices fell, the hedge fund's sudden collapse rocked the industry https://www.bloomberg.com/... via @BW
At least when Lehman went bankrupt, people knew where to find them: https://www.cnbc.com/...

Liquidators for crypto hedge fund Three Arrows Capital obtained U.S. court permission on Tuesday to issue subpoenas and lay claim to the bankrupt Singapore-based company's assets, noting that 3AC's missing-in-action founders no longer control its accounts https://www.reuters.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Tale as old as time: “What surprises me most about 3AC's collapse was how they were able to amass so much leverage in the first place” https://www.bloomberg.com/... via @BW

“They might have been stupid and misguided .. But they really believe this stuff, and you can see it in their books, right? You wouldn't have traded this way, if you didn't believe that this was true.” #crypto ⁦@business⁩ https://www.bloomberg.com/...

Wrote about the rise and fall of Three Arrows Capital with @MuyaoShen and @BenBartenstein Between their big bank trading experience and crypto connections, everyone assumed they were pros. “They turned out to be degens like everyone else.” https://www.bloomberg.com/... @crypto @BW

Very odd Liquidators not exercising StarkWare warrants This might be explained by average Liquidator being old and surely not familiar with crypto, therefore not willing to pay [even a super low] strike price to convert to StarkWare tokens This is harmful to creditors tho.. https://twitter.com/...

Twitter's lawyers: This man is a complete joke! Also Twitter's lawyers: And that's why he should own Twitter.
Twitter sues Elon Musk, calling his attempts to weasel out of the deal “a model of hypocrisy” and “a model of bad faith.” https://t.co/iWpLK9h7Wf https://t.co/DJ0xQjQYyC
What is Musk's team doing with the firehose data? Seems out of scope for the mdau / spam issue https://twitter.com/...

Big ouch for A16Z, Sequoia, Ellison, etc: Musk's investors, all sophisticated market participants, made these commitments in the face of Musk's public statements regarding spam accounts, and knowing he had forsworn diligence.
obviously the poop emoji made it into the complaint https://twitter.com/...

Reading through this—and you should—made me realize Musk is trying to do with business norms what trump did to political norms. If he gets away with a slap on the wrist, then what does that say about all contracts? I mean the damage done to TWTR is massive and it ain't over yet. https://twitter.com/...

It really is wild to have a company spend 60+ pages bashing someone in a lawsuit while simultaneously demanding that person buy the company and become everybody's new boss. This situation is not good. https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

OK, here we are! Very first thing, confirming that indeed, Twitter is asking that the Court force Musk to buy Twitter at the contract price. Again, this case is *NOT* about a $1 billion termination fee. 1/ https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

Twitter's lead lawyer is Bill Savitt, co-chair of litigation at Wachtell Lipton and a top Delaware Chancery litigator. Brad Sorrels of Wilson Sonsini, another Delaware Chancery veteran, is also on the complaint. Local counsel is Potter Anderson & Corroon. https://twitter.com/...
Wow, just look how hard Musk has worked to understand Twitter's process for measuring spam https://twitter.com/...
full internal memo from Twitter ceo Parag Agrawal to employees this afternoon: (in response to an earlier widely sent email from general counsel Sean Edgett) https://twitter.com/...

I do still think it would be surprising if the court orders Musk to fully buy Twitter, but it's not impossible at this point and honestly Musk might be lucky if he can get out of this for just a few billion.

Why @Twitter? Did you hear the Twitter employee witness at the #January6thHearings today describing how your platform was used to foment a violent mob attack on our govt by the man Musk wants to invite back on? What are your full fiduciary responsibilities? https://twitter.com/...
The only silver lining of this case is the prospect of lawyers dragging out tortuously detailed explanations for Elon's meme posts in a courtroom https://www.theverge.com/...

this is such a good complaint by Twitter's Delaware lawyers. I was a public-company M&A atty at Weil Gotshal for 7.5 years & have a good sense of how these deals work...We'll need to see all Musk's evidence of Twitter's alleged breaches, but I think Musk might finally ‘lose’ .. https://twitter.com/...
Imagine the good that could otherwise be done with all the legal fees for this mess https://twitter.com/...

I claim no special knowledge or expertise or authority on tech issues. If anything, I'm badly in the red on these things. But I had a gut feeling this whole thing was a massive grift designed to drum up PR. All I've learned is Musk has a baseball team worth of kids... https://twitter.com/...

$TWTR just did Elon Musk a huge favor by filing today. Normally, Musk runs damage control by scheduling events ("launches" of products but perhaps also rockets) when bad news is scheduled to run. Today, Wachtell and WSGR (which also works for $TSLA) gave him cover for free.

Big picture, the Twitter suit really is one of the strangest documents I've ever read. The company is powerfully demonstrating the profound unseriousness of one person, and trying to force a person to buy them against his will. And it's the same person!!!!!!

I'll never forget the strange Muskrats screaming about how this was gonna be some kind of reckoning and all that's happened is Elon Musk losing money and continuing to lose money. https://twitter.com/...

If you've ever wanted a reason to believe capitalism is broken, Twitter saying “Elon Musk is a joke and a jerk and should also be forced to own our company” seems like a good place to start? https://www.theverge.com/...

@Kantrowitz Please then explain Twitter's rationale in filing this case. Wouldn't it make more sense to enforce billion dollar penalty, move on & hopefully stabilize the stock price? This case will make stock more volatile and cause them to lose even more. (And yes, aware of purchase price)
watch the supreme court rule that contracts are no longer real https://twitter.com/...

Interesting nugget from the Twitter lawsuit: Twitter accuses Musk of “delaying and stymying key operational decisions”, including by not giving the go ahead for the company to launch 2 employee retention programs (Per the agreement, Musk has to sign off on decisions like this) https://twitter.com/...

This lawsuit will be fascinating to watch. Many will start to understand why so many businesses (even African startups) register in Delaware. Court of Chancery is a “court of equity” that weighs more than law in rendering decisions and has deep understanding of business. https://twitter.com/...

Not sure why it's funny? Twitter has a duty to its shareholders. Specific performance is worth $16 billion to the shareholders compared to a breakup fee, and the contract expressly provides that as a remedy. No litigator is surprised by this. https://twitter.com/...

twitter ceo parag agrawal just sent out an internal memo basically aimed at calming the troops. “We took the time to defend our company, our people and our stockholders. We plan to hold the buyer fully accountable to fulfill his contractual obligations.”
Musk's data team got access to the Twitter firehouse and ran 100,000 queries in two weeks. Seems like a lot. https://twitter.com/...
this is no dry legal brief almost as if it was meant to be tweeted and as a friend noted, aimed at winning both in the Court of Public Opinion as well as Delaware Chancery https://twitter.com/...
We will force this man unfit to run the company to buy the company for the good of the company https://twitter.com/...

Hmm! When Twitter gave Elon Musk its firehouse data, he and his team ran *search* queries, like any user would search terms, it sounds like. He pretended this was about bots. But what if he was searching for something else in the firehose data? https://twitter.com/...

Reminder: The Twitter complaint is just Twitter's version of events. The whole point is to make Twitter look good and Elon look not good. Obviously Elon will have a counter worthy of his super genius. If you thought his tweets were impressive... just wait! https://twitter.com/...

Sounds like Twitter's legal team is hoping to get this to trial as early as mid-September and believes the trial can be done in just 4 days (!), according to a source. Ambitious! regardless, they clearly want to move fast. https://twitter.com/...

The Twitter suit is blistering and just ... obviously true? You can't buy a distressed property and brag to neighbors about how you're going to fix the horrible termite problem ... and then cry after signing bc you're astonished to find termites in the walk-through. https://twitter.com/...
That's a strong complaint. They seek a sole remedy - specific performance. Obviously other remedies could emerge but for now, they seek one. https://twitter.com/...

What's funny about Twitter's lawsuit against El*n is that they're NOT looking for the $1 bn breakup fee. Weirdly, Twitter's argument is that Elon has done so much damage to the company that he should acquire it. Essentially, “you break it, you buy it.” https://www.documentcloud.org/ ... https://twitter.com/...

TWITTER not mincing words: “Musk apparently believes that he— unlike every other party subject to Delaware contract law — is free to change his mind, trash the company, disrupt its operations, destroy stockholder value, and walk away.”

Suit on bots: Elon's “information requests were designed to try to tank the deal. Musk's increasingly outlandish requests reflect not a genuine examination of Twitter's processes but a litigation-driven campaign to try to create a record of non-cooperation on Twitter's part.”
In this lawsuit, Twitter finds a lot of ways to call Musk a liar. Among vocabulary used: “pretending,” “conjure,” “asserted, falsely,” “bad faith.”
As expected, Twitter is asking for “specific performance” — for Elon Musk to go through with his contractual agreement to buy Twitter. https://t.co/b8yA9grEkn

They're not wrong here, but if they're doing this out of fiduciary duty, then fiduciary duty is deeply flawed. There's no way any of this anything but incredibly stupid. Make this all go away as soon as possible so staff and the company can have some certainty. https://t.co/C8SUyfph0Z

Twitter calling him “a model of hypocrisy” in a lawsuit to get him to buy the company... is just all-around hilarious and keeping in complete character with Twitter, actually https://www.theverge.com/...

Quick takeaways from @twitter lawsuit: -Alleges Musk strategy “a model of bad faith” & “model of hypocrisy” -Alleges he “violated his obligations to treat requests for consent reasonably” -Says his disparaging public comments created “business risk” & “downward pressure” on stock https://twitter.com/...
“Twitter also negotiated for itself a right to hire and fire employees at all levels, including executives, without having to seek Musk's consent.”

“For Musk, it would seem, Twitter, the interests of its stockholders, the transaction Musk agreed to, and the court process to enforce it all constitute an elaborate joke.” https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ... https://twitter.com/...

As a longtime Medium contributor, I find this news interesting, though not necessarily concerning. Medium has always had potential but it needs to choose its course and stick to it. https://www.nytimes.com/...

First off - congratulations to Ev. I read articles on Medium every single day. Every day. So many voices get a chance to be heard and discovered. And the business has come a long way too. What he has built and run for a decade is fantastic and I am glad to see new roads ahead https://twitter.com/...

Sad about this but I am grateful to @ev for once again—after Blogger and Twitter—giving more people more ways to speak online. He is a quiet visionary and I look forward to what comes next. Evan Williams Is Stepping Down as C.E.O. of Medium https://www.nytimes.com/...

Wow! The CEOs of the largest companies and nonprofits in the United States have joined forces to make computer science a basic part of the K-12 curriculum. See who's on board: https://ceosforcs.com/

Coding is one of the most valuable skills a person can learn. It can open new doors, jumpstart careers, and help big dreams seem like achievable goals. Everyone around the world should have an opportunity to learn how to code. https://www.ceosforcs.com/

When I was 13, computer science changed the course of my life. I was really lucky to have access to a computer that early on. I hope this initiative will give every student the same opportunity. https://twitter.com/...

Today, I join 500 business and nonprofit leaders to call for every student in every school to have the opportunity to learn computer science as a basic part of education. I encourage other leaders to sign too, to better prepare our children for the future. https://www.ceosforcs.com/

We agree with @Apple CEO @tim_cook . Being a father, and having been blessed with a strong education, we believe it's time for a renaissance in education. We must invest in teachers + restructure our curriculum to empower everyone. Coding is the great equalizer. Our teachers can. https://twitter.com/...

Computer science opened up a world of opportunity for me. Every student should have the chance to learn one of our generation's superpowers. Even if it's just to make video games :) You can help K-12 students across America have more oppty via @codeorg https://www.ceosforcs.com/
This is a huge deal. I love how https://code.org/ is always leading the charge with educating the future leaders in tech. https://twitter.com/...
Computational Philosophy, taught through Computer science, is a required foundational skill of the modern world. Let's make it available to everyone. https://www.ceosforcs.com/

Computer science is essential for almost every career. Every student in every school should have the chance to learn these skills. I'm proud Microsoft's @satyanadella and @BradSmi are signatories, alongside 500 CEOs and leaders across the nation. https://www.ceosforcs.com/ @codeorg
This was the definition of “no brainer” to sign on to. #ceosforcs https://twitter.com/...

Today I join 500 business and nonprofit leaders to call for every student in every school to have the opportunity to learn computer science as a basic part of education. I encourage other leaders to sign, to better prepare our children for the future. https://www.ceosforcs.com/.

Computer science is essential for almost every career in today's world. I believe every student in every school should have the chance to learn it, which is why I added my name to this letter, alongside 500 other CEOs and leaders across the nation. https://www.ceosforcs.com/

As an innovation-driven company, @UPS knows how important #technology is in moving our world forward. I'm proud to join @Codeorg's call for greater access to computer science education to prepare students for success not just in tech, but in almost any career. https://twitter.com/...

It's critical to better prepare the next generation for their future jobs. Alongside @codeorg and more than 500 CEOs, I'm honored to advocate for all students to have the opportunity to learn computer science and pursue careers in tech and beyond. https://www.ceosforcs.com/

Computer science is a fundamental part of education, and that's why I joined more than 500 other leaders to call for every student to have the opportunity to learn computer science as a basic part of education. Learn more: https://www.ceosforcs.com/

@TechBushPro @techflowtime It's honestly really cool for the ringtone effect. Kind of like how you can enable the flash on iPhone to light up when you get a call or notification. Except now, it's customizable and a conversation starter.

So is this much ado about Nothing? Yes and no. The Nothing (1) does not — cannot — live up to the hype it's gotten. It is not a threat to BBK/Xiaomi/Samsung/Apple. However, it might be interesting enough to sell in moderate volumes and create future opportunities. https://twitter.com/...
£399 is a good price for a new smartphone, even if it doesn't have true flagship specs. Interested to see how Nothing does across Europe https://twitter.com/...
